page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.medium is a general-purpose server powered by an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. This instance features 1 dedicated physical core and 1 vCPU operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. No local storage or GPU accelerators are bundled with this instance, and it relies on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the single-core capabilities of the Neoverse-V2 processor are moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores landing in the average tier. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly, reaching 14.2 GB/sec. However, because the instance is limited to a single vCPU, multi-core and multi-threaded benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, are rated as poor. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with uncached read and write speeds categorized as weak and poor respectively.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers an entry-level hardware profile. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that do not require parallel processing or high memory throughput. The absence of local storage and dedicated GPUs, combined with a 2 Gbps network baseline, makes it less suitable for high-performance computing, large-scale databases, or real-time LLM inference.

The m8gb.medium is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads. Appropriate applications include basic web serving, simple text processing, small-scale background utility scripts, and low-traffic development or testing environments that do not demand high multi-core performance or massive memory bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.medium is a general-purpose server featuring a single-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 1 dedicated vCPU, 4.0 GB of system memory, and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are low due to the single-vCPU limitation. This entry-level instance provides a qualitative balance of resources for lightweight, single-threaded tasks. It is best suited for basic web serving, text processing, and low-traffic development environments.

tagline: An entry-level ARM64 instance featuring a dedicated single-core processor and balanced memory for lightweight, single-threaded utility workloads.
